title: Intent classified redundantly -- up to 3x per routeQuery invocation
evidence: classifyIntent(query).intent is called inside shouldPreserveBm25 (line 145), shouldPreserveGraph (line 191), and directly in routeQuery (line 304). For a simple-tier find_decision query, the intent classifier runs 3 times for the same input.
scopeProof: grep -n classifyIntent query-router.ts confirms the function is called only within routeQuery call chain
recommendation: Pass pre-computed intent from routeQuery into shouldPreserveBm25 and shouldPreserveGraph as optional parameter

title: ChannelName type duplicated from query-router.ts
evidence: Literal union type ChannelName defined identically in routing-telemetry.ts:14 and query-router.ts:35
scopeProof: rg -n 'type ChannelName' routing-telemetry.ts query-router.ts shows two definitions
recommendation: Import ChannelName from query-router.ts or extract to shared channels module

title: recordInvocation uses Array.shift() not true ring buffer
evidence: recentDecisions.shift() is O(n) per overflow event. Doc describes as ring buffer but implementation is array-based FIFO.
scopeProof: rg -n 'shift\(\)' routing-telemetry.ts confirms single usage pattern
recommendation: Replace with fixed-size array + write-index cursor or rename to rolling window

title: shouldPreserveGraph does not self-gate on feature flag
evidence: SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION flag only checked in routeQuery (line 325), not in exported shouldPreserveGraph (line 183). Direct callers bypass the flag.
scopeProof: rg -n 'isGraphChannelPreservationEnabled' query-router.ts shows only one call site at line 325
recommendation: Either make shouldPreserveGraph module-private or add flag check inside function

title: No isolated try/catch around getRoutingTelemetrySnapshot in memory_health
evidence: getRoutingTelemetrySnapshot() called unguarded at line 626 inside the health handler full-report path. If telemetry module throws unexpectedly, entire handleMemoryHealth response fails.
scopeProof: rg -n 'getRoutingTelemetrySnapshot' memory-crud-health.ts shows single unguarded call site
recommendation: Wrap in try/catch with fallback to zero-value routing snapshot

detail: P0=0 blockers: no broken correctness, security exploits, data loss, undefined behavior, or missing spec requirements
P1=1 major: redundant intent classification (P1-001) -- low-impact performance concern, not correctness-blocking
P2=4 minor: type duplication (P2-001), array-shift idiom (P2-002), flag self-gating gap (P2-003), missing defensive guard (P2-004)
Traceability core PASS: all 8 REQs traceable spec->task->code->test->checklist. Overlay PARTIAL: feature_catalog_code and playbook_capability entries exist but not content-audited. Resource map N/A.

reason: Query uses parameterized placeholder (?) for HAVING threshold; table names memory_index and causal_edges are hardcoded string literals -- no user-input interpolation

direction: Race condition in entity-density cache swap
reason: JavaScript assignment is single-threaded atomic for variable rebind; buildIndex runs synchronously before cachedTerms assignment; no concurrent-read window

direction: shouldPreserveGraph intent+entity-density interaction producing wrong includeDegree
reason: Intent path sets preserved=true, includeDegree stays false. Entity-density path overwrites includeDegree=true only when its threshold fires. Both paths independently correct -- when both fire, includeDegree is correctly true per spec decision

direction: bm25+graph override order causing channel loss
reason: Both overrides use enforceMinimumChannels([...adjustedChannels, ...additions]) which is additive only; no channel removal paths exist; order is irrelevant
